supply_chain.dependencies: Hardware products manufactured by third-party contract manufacturers located outside the U.S., primarily in China. Limited number of component and product suppliers for hardware devices.
supply_chain.risks: Reliance on single or limited sources of supply for key hardware components. Risk of shortage, price increases, tariffs, changes, delay or discontinuation of key components. Supply chain disruptions including strikes, port shutdowns, intellectual property theft, quality control issues, and geopolitical risks.

regulatory_risks: Extensive regulation and oversight in payments, money transmission, lending, deposit-taking, cross-border money transmission, foreign exchange, cryptocurrency, trading in shares, consumer protection, anti-money laundering, anti-bribery, anti-corruption, sanctions regimes and export controls, AI, privacy, data protection and cybersecurity
State and federal money transmitter licensing requirements and compliance obligations
Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) regulation and enforcement actions
BNPL regulatory scrutiny and potential new regulations
SEC and FINRA regulation of Cash App Investing as a broker-dealer, including net capital requirements
FDIC regulation of Square Financial Services (industrial loan company) including capital requirements and source of financial strength obligations
Virtual currency regulation including New York BitLicense and evolving state and federal cryptocurrency regulations
GDPR and UK data protection regulations with penalties up to 4% of worldwide annual revenue or €20 million/£17.5 million
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and similar state privacy laws
Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) compliance
Potential changes to interchange fees and assessment fees by payment card networks or government regulation
Tax regulations including OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax, state gross receipts taxes, digital services taxes, and reporting requirements
Climate-related disclosure requirements including SEC rules and California climate laws
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and UK Bribery Act compliance
Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctions compliance
Bank Secrecy Act / Anti-Money Laundering program requirements
Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) compliance for certain products
Evolving AI regulations and governance requirements

sanction_risks: Risk of processing transactions with sanctioned persons or entities
Exposure to nation-state cyber-attacks related to Russia's invasion of Ukraine and other geopolitical conflicts
Potential impact of tariffs on hardware products manufactured in China (25% tariffs on some devices, 7.5% on others)
Risk of increased or new tariffs as proposed by new U.S. presidential administration
